author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Donatist Controversy.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
In which Augustin replies to all the several statements in the letter of Petilianus, as though disputing with an adversary face to face.

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Petilianus said: "Paul also, the apostle, whilst he was suffering fearful persecutions at the hands of all nations, endured even more grievous troubles at the hands of false brethren, as he bears witness of himself, being oftentimes afflicted: ‘In perils by the heathen, in perils by mine own countrymen, in perils among false brethren.’

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: And again he says, ‘Be ye followers of me, even as I also am of Christ.’

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: When, therefore, false brethren like yourselves assault us, we imitate the patience of our master Paul under our dangers."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: Certainly those of whom you speak are false brethren, of whom the apostle thus complains in another place, where he is extolling the natural sincerity of Timothy: "I have no man," he says, "like-minded, who will naturally care for your state.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: For all seek their own, not the things which are Jesus Christ’s."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Undoubtedly he was speaking of those who were with him at the time when he was writing that epistle; for it could not be that all Christians in every quarter of the earth were seeking their own, and not the things which were Jesus Christ’s.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: It was of those, therefore, as I said, who were with him at the time when he was writing the words which you have quoted, that he uttered this lamentation.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: For who else was it to whom he referred, when he says in another place, "Without were fightings, within were fears," except those whom he feared all the more intensely because they were within?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: If, therefore, you would imitate Paul, you would be tolerant of false brethren within, not a slanderer of the innocent without.
